reviews.review_msg: Noel's exams are basic. Participation marks are a big part of your grade. He asks cryptic open-ended questions with specific answers that are hard to guess, but you get used to it. The readings and course content make up for somewhat lackluster lectures. You could do a lot worse than a prof that's a little boring. The workload is not heavy.

The readings and course content make up for somewhat lackluster lectures. You could do a lot worse than a prof that's a little boring. The workload is not heavy.

The readings and course content make up for somewhat lackluster lectures. You could do a lot worse than a prof that's a little boring. The workload is not heavy.

Hard work pays off in his classes. Lots of readings, but from a very good selection. He is funny and witty. Lectures are never boring. Very respectful and approachable. Lectures are engaging. He loves what he does and his energy is always great! So is his music playlists!

Hard work pays off in his classes. Lots of readings, but from a very good selection. He is funny and witty. Lectures are never boring. Very respectful and approachable. Lectures are engaging. He loves what he does and his energy is always great! So is his music playlists!

Hard work pays off in his classes. Lots of readings, but from a very good selection. He is funny and witty. Lectures are never boring. Very respectful and approachable. Lectures are engaging. He loves what he does and his energy is always great! So is his music playlists!
A lovely prof with a genuine interest in the material and in his students. Docks marks for not participating in class discussion and missing class, so be aware. Is very fair and engaging.

Great guy.  Doesn't come with a regular professor's demeanor and instead is more relaxed.  Class usually dictates the direction of discussion, though he prompts it.  He doesn't give back marks till the end except for weekly annotations, but he is more than fair with grading.  Average workload, relaxed guy, fair marker - it couldn't get much better.

Hands down one of my favourite SA professors! I have taken SA 319 with him previously and thoroughly enjoyed it! Class discussions are interesting but can go off topic sometimes. Other than that, he is extremely helpful and a fair marker! Would definitely recommend!

He is absolute eye-candy, and has a very sexy accent. Efe really cares of your success and encourages office hour discussions. The problem I had with him is I didn't know how I was doing in the course until the final grades were given. Efe never returned our papers, so I think many students were shocked with their marks. He means well, though.

Cristina is really kind. Her lectures are easy to follow and understand as long as you have done the readings. Besides this, this is a very tough marker and provides little criteria making it difficult to get a good grade. Shes nice, but I dont see myself taking another class with her.

I love Cristina. She's so sweet and genuinely cares about her students. I took SA364 with her and decided to take another SA course the following semester because she a great a prof. Attendance was mandatory and every class had a writing response activity although it was more of a pass/fail basis and basically was done for attendance.

I have absolutely nothing against this professor as a person; she is very sweet and caring, and really cares about student wellbeing. I just had a really tough time following what this class was trying to explain. Lectures were a bit confusing and unstructured, and involved a lot of group activities that didn't always seem to have relevance.

Christina is approachable and seems to have a high interest in the subject. She likes talking about Milan a lot! Her class arrangement is decent and the four hours were divided well with a variety of class discussion, lectures and video. However, there is a lack of clarity with the assignments and I would like to have learnt more about Anthropology
